I need to understand what these conditions are doing. The XOR operation (^) between two numbers gives a result where each bit is set if the corresponding bits of operands are different. So, instant ^ sum will have bits set where instant and sum differ. 

The original condition throws an exception when (instant ^ sum) < 0, which in two's complement terms means that the highest bit is set, indicating negative numbers. This suggests that adding the offset caused a wrap-around or overflow.
